title: holcus lanatus subsp. vaginatus
intro: Holcus lanatus subsp. vaginatus, also known as common velvet grass, is a perennial grass species that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of common velvet grass include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential antifungal and antibacterial properties. Common velvet grass can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves.
